title: Amazon River
content: The Amazon River is the largest river by discharge volume of water in the world. It is approximately 6,400 km (4,000 miles) long. The Nile River is generally considered the longest river at approximately 6,650 km, though some measurements place the Amazon as longer. The Amazon basin covers about 7 million square kilometers.

title: Mount Everest
content: Mount Everest is the highest mountain above sea level at 8,848.86 meters (29,031.7 feet), as measured by China and Nepal in 2020. It is located in the Mahalangur Himal sub-range of the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and Tibet. The first confirmed summit was by Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay on 29 May 1953.

title: Sahara Desert
content: The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world, covering approximately 9.2 million square kilometers (3.6 million square miles) in North Africa. It is the third largest desert overall after Antarctica and the Arctic. The Sahara spans 11 countries including Algeria, Chad, Egypt, Libya, Mali, Mauritania, Morocco, Niger, Western Sahara, Sudan, and Tunisia.

title: Great Barrier Reef
content: The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system, stretching over 2,300 kilometers (1,430 miles) along the northeast coast of Australia. It is composed of over 2,900 individual reefs and 900 islands. It was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1981.

title: Population of Countries
content: As of 2024, India is the most populous country with approximately 1.44 billion people, having surpassed China (approximately 1.42 billion) in 2023. The United States is third with approximately 340 million. Indonesia is fourth with approximately 277 million. Pakistan is fifth with approximately 240 million.

title: Mariana Trench
content: The Mariana Trench is the deepest known oceanic trench, located in the western Pacific Ocean. Its deepest point, the Challenger Deep, reaches approximately 10,935 meters (35,876 feet) below sea level. Jacques Piccard and Don Walsh were the first to reach the bottom in 1960 in the bathyscaphe Trieste. James Cameron made a solo descent in 2012.

title: European Union
content: The European Union was formally established by the Maastricht Treaty in 1993. As of 2024, it has 27 member states after the United Kingdom left on 31 January 2020 (Brexit). The EU covers an area of 4.2 million square kilometers with a population of about 450 million people. The euro is used by 20 of the 27 member states.

title: Continents and Area
content: The seven continents by area are: Asia (44.58 million km²), Africa (30.37 million km²), North America (24.71 million km²), South America (17.84 million km²), Antarctica (14.2 million km²), Europe (10.18 million km²), and Australia/Oceania (8.53 million km²). Asia is the largest and most populous continent.

title: Nile River
content: The Nile River is generally considered the longest river in the world at approximately 6,650 km (4,130 miles). It flows northward through northeastern Africa, primarily through Sudan and Egypt, emptying into the Mediterranean Sea. The Nile has two major tributaries: the White Nile and the Blue Nile.

title: Capital Cities
content: The capital of Australia is Canberra (not Sydney or Melbourne). The capital of Brazil is Brasília (not Rio de Janeiro or São Paulo). The capital of Turkey is Ankara (not Istanbul). The capital of Myanmar is Naypyidaw (not Yangon). The capital of Nigeria is Abuja (not Lagos). These are commonly confused facts.
